title: Helia glabra
taxonomy: Helia glabra is classified under the family Fabaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is a legume native to tropical Africa. The plant is often referred to by its synonym, Abrus precatorius, which is commonly known as the rosary pea or crab's eye. The classification of Helia glabra is further divided into the genus Abrus, which contains several other species of flowering plants. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics.
uses: Helia glabra, also known as the Smooth Heliotrope, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating skin conditions and wounds. In some parts of the world, it's used to treat fever and as a general tonic. The plant's roots and leaves are often used in herbal remedies, and it's also believed to have antibacterial properties. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of Helia glabra's medicinal uses.

parts: Helia glabra, also known as the smooth heliotrope, is a plant that has been utilized for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are often used in aromatherapy and skincare products due to their purported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. side_effects: Helia glabra, also known as Smooth Heliotrope, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
